title: WebNami vs Ghost — A Free, Simpler Blogging Alternative
description: Compare WebNami and Ghost. WebNami is free, file-based, and needs no Node.js server to run in production. Own your content without a monthly bill.

hero.heading: All the simplicity of Ghost, without the hosting bill
hero.subheading: Ghost is a polished blogging platform but charges $9–$199/month for hosting, and self-hosting requires a Node.js server and a database. WebNami gives you a clean writing experience and a static site you can host anywhere for free.

reasons.heading: Zero hosting costs
reasons.body: Ghost Pro starts at $9/month. WebNami generates a static site you can host on Netlify, Vercel, or GitHub Pages — for free, forever.

reasons.heading: Files, not databases
reasons.body: Ghost stores content in a database. WebNami stores it as Markdown files you can open in any editor, back up with Git, and move anywhere without an export step.

reasons.heading: No server to maintain
reasons.body: Self-hosted Ghost needs Node.js, a process manager, and regular upgrades. WebNami's output is static HTML — deploy it and forget about runtime maintenance.

reasons.heading: Faster pages out of the box
reasons.body: Ghost renders pages on each request. WebNami pre-builds everything into static HTML so every visitor gets an instant response with no server processing time.
